title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement John Zobolas. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired [Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder][https://github.com/mozilla/inclusion]. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Contributing

what: Efficient Voting Methods for Committee Selection
title: Efficient Voting Methods for Committee Selection
text: Package website fastVoteR R package efficient voting methods committee selection. Uses Rcpp.

title: Efficient Voting Methods for Committee Selection
text: CRAN version: Development version GitHub:

title: Efficient Voting Methods for Committee Selection
code: library(fastVoteR)  # 5 candidates candidates = paste0("V", seq_len(5)) candidates #> [1] "V1" "V2" "V3" "V4" "V5"  # 4 voters with their preferences (approval ballots) voters = list(   c("V3", "V1", "V4"),   c("V3", "V1"),   c("V3", "V2"),   c("V2", "V4") )  set.seed(42)  # voter weights weights = c(1.1, 2.5, 0.8, 0.9)  # Approval voting (all voters equal) rank_candidates(voters, candidates) #>    candidate score norm_score borda_score #>       <char> <num>      <num>       <num> #> 1:        V3     3       0.75        1.00 #> 2:        V1     2       0.50        0.75 #> 3:        V2     2       0.50        0.50 #> 4:        V4     2       0.50        0.25 #> 5:        V5     0       0.00        0.00  # Approval voting (voters unequal) rank_candidates(voters, candidates, weights) #>    candidate score norm_score borda_score #>       <char> <num>      <num>       <num> #> 1:        V3   4.4  0.8301887        1.00 #> 2:        V1   3.6  0.6792453        0.75 #> 3:        V4   2.0  0.3773585        0.50 #> 4:        V2   1.7  0.3207547        0.25 #> 5:        V5   0.0  0.0000000        0.00  # Satisfaction Approval voting (voters unequal) rank_candidates(voters, candidates, weights, method = "sav") #>    candidate     score norm_score borda_score #>       <char>     <num>      <num>       <num> #> 1:        V3 2.0166667  0.8175676        1.00 #> 2:        V1 1.6166667  0.6554054        0.75 #> 3:        V2 0.8500000  0.3445946        0.50 #> 4:        V4 0.8166667  0.3310811        0.25 #> 5:        V5 0.0000000  0.0000000        0.00  # Sequential Proportional Approval voting (voters equal) rank_candidates(voters, candidates, method = "seq_pav") #>    candidate borda_score #>       <char>       <num> #> 1:        V3        1.00 #> 2:        V2        0.75 #> 3:        V1        0.50 #> 4:        V4        0.25 #> 5:        V5        0.00

title: Efficient Voting Methods for Committee Selection
text: See vote votesys R packages. strictly ABC-voting rules, see abcvoting Python package.

title: Efficient Voting Methods for Committee Selection
text: Please note fastVoteR project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project, agree abide terms.

title: Approval Voting — av
text: Approval Voting (AV) ranks candidates based number voters approving . function uses internal C++ implementation efficient computation. equal weights, faster R implementation used.

title: Approval Voting — av
code: av(   voters,   candidates,   weights = NULL,   committee_size = NULL,   borda_score = TRUE,   check = FALSE )

title: Approval Voting — av
text: voters (list())  list subsets (character vectors), subset contains candidates approved selected voter. candidates (character())  vector candidates ranked. weights (numeric()|NULL)  numeric vector non-negative weights representing voter's influence. Larger weight, higher influence. Must length voters. NULL (default), voters assigned equal weights 1, representing equal influence. committee_size (integer(1)|NULL) Number top candidates include ranking. Default (NULL) includes candidates. borda_score (logical(1))  Whether include borda_score column output, provides normalized score based candidate's rank. TRUE (default), borda_score calculated \((p - ) / (p - 1)\), \(p\) total number candidates \(\) candidate's rank. check (logical(1))  Whether run additional voter-integrity checks. TRUE, voter must approve least one candidate, approvals must unique per voter, approved candidates must appear candidates. Use FALSE skip checks inputs known valid.

title: Approval Voting — av
text: data.frame columns: "candidate": Candidate names. "score": Approval scores. "norm_score": Normalized scores, scaled range \([0,1]\). "borda_score": Borda scores method-agnostic comparison, ranging \([0,1]\), top candidate receives score 1 lowest-ranked candidate receives score 0, based total number candidates. Candidates ordered decreasing "score".

what: fastVoteR: Efficient Voting Methods for Committee Selection — fastVoteR-package
title: fastVoteR: Efficient Voting Methods for Committee Selection — fastVoteR-package
text: fast 'Rcpp'-based implementation polynomially-computable voting theory methods committee ranking scoring. package includes methods Approval Voting (AV), Satisfaction Approval Voting (SAV), sequential Proportional Approval Voting (PAV), sequential Phragmen's Rule. Weighted variants methods also provided, allowing differential voter influence.

title: fastVoteR: Efficient Voting Methods for Committee Selection — fastVoteR-package
text: Maintainer: John Zobolas bblodfon@gmail.com (ORCID) contributors: Anne-Marie George (ORCID) [contributor]

what: Rank candidates based on voter preferences — rank_candidates
title: Rank candidates based on voter preferences — rank_candidates
text: Calculates ranking candidates based voters' preferences. Approval-Based Committe (ABC) rules based Lackner et al. (2023). example use Machine Learning ensemble feature selection, see Zobolas et al. (2026).

title: Rank candidates based on voter preferences — rank_candidates
code: rank_candidates(   voters,   candidates,   weights = NULL,   committee_size = NULL,   method = "av",   borda_score = TRUE,   shuffle_candidates = TRUE,   check = FALSE )

title: Rank candidates based on voter preferences — rank_candidates
text: voters (list())  list subsets (character vectors), subset contains candidates approved selected voter. candidates (character())  vector candidates ranked. weights (numeric()|NULL)  numeric vector non-negative weights representing voter's influence. Larger weight, higher influence. Must length voters. NULL (default), voters assigned equal weights 1, representing equal influence. committee_size (integer(1)|NULL) Number top candidates include ranking. Default (NULL) includes candidates. method (character(1))  ranking voting method use. Must one : "av", "sav", "seq_pav", "seq_phragmen". See Details. borda_score (logical(1))  Whether include borda_score column output, provides normalized score based candidate's rank. TRUE (default), borda_score calculated \((p - ) / (p - 1)\), \(p\) total number candidates \(\) candidate's rank. shuffle_candidates (logical(1))  Whether randomly shuffle candidates ranking. provides random tie-breaking avoids deterministic bias scores equal. Default TRUE. check (logical(1))  Whether run additional voter-integrity checks. TRUE, voter must approve least one candidate, approvals must unique per voter, approved candidates must appear candidates. Use FALSE skip checks inputs known valid.

title: Rank candidates based on voter preferences — rank_candidates
text: data.frame columns: "candidate": Candidate names. "score": Scores assigned candidate based selected method (applicable). "norm_score": Normalized scores (applicable), scaled range \([0,1]\), can loosely interpreted selection probabilities (see Meinshausen et al. (2010) example Machine Learning research goal perform stable feature selection). "borda_score": Borda scores method-agnostic comparison, ranging \([0,1]\), top candidate receives score 1 lowest-ranked candidate receives score 0. Candidates ordered decreasing "score", "borda_score" method returns rankings.

title: Rank candidates based on voter preferences — rank_candidates
text: implement several consensus-based ranking methods, voters express preferences candidates. framework three components: Voters: list element set approved candidates single voter. Candidates: character vector possible candidates. vector can shuffled randomize tie-breaking across methods. Weights: numeric vector giving voter’s influence. Equal weights mean equal influence; differing weights reflect varying importance. function thin wrapper dispatches method-specific implementations. Supported methods include: Approval Voting (method = "av"), calls av() Satisfaction Approval Voting (method = "sav"), calls sav() Sequential Proportional Approval Voting (method = "seq_pav"), calls seq_pav() Sequential Phragmen’s Rule (method = "seq_phragmen"), calls seq_phragmen() methods support voter weights. method-agnostic comparisons, compute borda scores, map candidate’s rank normalized scale across methods may otherwise return different score scales ordinal rankings. sequential methods "seq_pav" "seq_phragmen", committee_size parameter can speed computation selecting top \(N\) candidates instead producing full ranking. non-sequential methods (e.g., "sav" "av"), simply truncates final ranking top \(N\) candidates.

title: Rank candidates based on voter preferences — rank_candidates
text: Lackner M, Skowron P (2023). Multi-Winner Voting Approval Preferences. Springer Nature, 121 p. doi:10.1007/978-3-031-09016-5 . Zobolas J, George , Lopez , Fischer S, Becker M, Aittokallio T (2026). "Prognostic biomarker discovery pancreatic cancer hybrid ensemble feature selection multi-omics data." BioData Mining. doi:10.1186/s13040-026-00546-0 . Meinshausen N, Buhlmann P (2010). "Stability Selection." Journal Royal Statistical Society Series B: Statistical Methodology, 72(4), 417-473. doi:10.1111/J.1467-9868.2010.00740.X .

title: Rank candidates based on voter preferences — rank_candidates
code: # 5 candidates candidates = paste0("V", seq_len(5))  # 4 voters voters = list(   c("V3", "V1", "V4"),   c("V3", "V1"),   c("V3", "V2"),   c("V2", "V4") )  # voter weights weights = c(1.1, 2.5, 0.8, 0.9)  # Approval voting (all voters equal) rank_candidates(voters, candidates) #>   candidate score norm_score borda_score #> 1        V3     3       0.75        1.00 #> 2        V1     2       0.50        0.75 #> 3        V2     2       0.50        0.50 #> 4        V4     2       0.50        0.25 #> 5        V5     0       0.00        0.00  # Approval voting (voters unequal) rank_candidates(voters, candidates, weights) #>   candidate score norm_score borda_score #> 1        V3   4.4  0.8301887        1.00 #> 2        V1   3.6  0.6792453        0.75 #> 3        V4   2.0  0.3773585        0.50 #> 4        V2   1.7  0.3207547        0.25 #> 5        V5   0.0  0.0000000        0.00  # Satisfaction Approval voting (voters unequal, no borda score) rank_candidates(voters, candidates, weights, method = "sav", borda_score = FALSE) #>   candidate     score norm_score #> 1        V3 2.0166667  0.8175676 #> 2        V1 1.6166667  0.6554054 #> 3        V2 0.8500000  0.3445946 #> 4        V4 0.8166667  0.3310811 #> 5        V5 0.0000000  0.0000000  # Sequential Proportional Approval voting (voters equal, no borda score) rank_candidates(voters, candidates, method = "seq_pav", borda_score = FALSE) #>   candidate #> 1        V3 #> 2        V4 #> 3        V2 #> 4        V1 #> 5        V5  # Sequential Phragmen's Rule (voters equal) rank_candidates(voters, candidates, method = "seq_phragmen", borda_score = FALSE) #>   candidate #> 1        V3 #> 2        V2 #> 3        V1 #> 4        V4 #> 5        V5

title: Satisfaction Approval Voting — sav
text: Satisfaction Approval Voting (SAV) ranks candidates normalizing approval scores based size voter's approval set. Voters approve candidates contribute lesser score individual approved candidates. function uses internal C++ implementation efficient computation.

title: Satisfaction Approval Voting — sav
code: sav(   voters,   candidates,   weights = NULL,   committee_size = NULL,   borda_score = TRUE,   check = FALSE )

title: Satisfaction Approval Voting — sav
text: voters (list())  list subsets (character vectors), subset contains candidates approved selected voter. candidates (character())  vector candidates ranked. weights (numeric()|NULL)  numeric vector non-negative weights representing voter's influence. Larger weight, higher influence. Must length voters. NULL (default), voters assigned equal weights 1, representing equal influence. committee_size (integer(1)|NULL) Number top candidates include ranking. Default (NULL) includes candidates. borda_score (logical(1))  Whether include borda_score column output, provides normalized score based candidate's rank. TRUE (default), borda_score calculated \((p - ) / (p - 1)\), \(p\) total number candidates \(\) candidate's rank. check (logical(1))  Whether run additional voter-integrity checks. TRUE, voter must approve least one candidate, approvals must unique per voter, approved candidates must appear candidates. Use FALSE skip checks inputs known valid.

title: Satisfaction Approval Voting — sav
text: data.frame columns: "candidate": Candidate names. "score": Satisfaction scores. "norm_score": Normalized scores, scaled range \([0,1]\). "borda_score": Borda scores method-agnostic comparison, ranging \([0,1]\), top candidate receives score 1 lowest-ranked candidate receives score 0, based total number candidates. Candidates ordered decreasing "score".

title: Sequential Proportional Approval Voting — seq_pav
text: Sequential Proportional Approval Voting (SeqPAV) multi-winner method builds committee iteratively maximizing proportionality-based satisfaction score. selection, weights voters approved chosen candidate reduced, promotes proportional representation. PAV score computed weighted sum harmonic numbers based many elected candidates voter supports. process continues specified committee size reached candidates ranked. function uses internal C++ implementation efficient computation.

title: Sequential Proportional Approval Voting — seq_pav
code: seq_pav(   voters,   candidates,   weights = NULL,   committee_size = NULL,   borda_score = TRUE,   check = FALSE )

title: Sequential Proportional Approval Voting — seq_pav
text: voters (list())  list subsets (character vectors), subset contains candidates approved selected voter. candidates (character())  vector candidates ranked. weights (numeric()|NULL)  numeric vector non-negative weights representing voter's influence. Larger weight, higher influence. Must length voters. NULL (default), voters assigned equal weights 1, representing equal influence. committee_size (integer(1)|NULL) Number top candidates include ranking. Default (NULL) includes candidates. borda_score (logical(1))  Whether include borda_score column output, provides normalized score based candidate's rank. TRUE (default), borda_score calculated \((p - ) / (p - 1)\), \(p\) total number candidates \(\) candidate's rank. check (logical(1))  Whether run additional voter-integrity checks. TRUE, voter must approve least one candidate, approvals must unique per voter, approved candidates must appear candidates. Use FALSE skip checks inputs known valid.

title: Sequential Proportional Approval Voting — seq_pav
text: data.frame columns: "candidate": Candidate names. "borda_score": Borda scores method-agnostic comparison, ranging \([0,1]\), top candidate receives score 1 lowest-ranked candidate receives score 0, based total number candidates. Candidates ordered sequence selected.

title: Sequential Phragmen's Rule — seq_phragmen
text: Sequential Phragmen's Rule multi-winner method builds committee distributing representation loads across voters evenly possible. step, selects candidate yields smallest increase voter load, updates loads voters approved chosen candidate. process continues committee filled candidates ranked. function uses internal C++ implementation efficient computation.

title: Sequential Phragmen's Rule — seq_phragmen
code: seq_phragmen(   voters,   candidates,   weights = NULL,   committee_size = NULL,   borda_score = TRUE,   check = FALSE )

Larger weight, higher influence. Must length voters. NULL (default), voters assigned equal weights 1, representing equal influence. committee_size (integer(1)|NULL) Number top candidates include ranking. Default (NULL) includes candidates. borda_score (logical(1))  Whether include borda_score column output, provides normalized score based candidate's rank. TRUE (default), borda_score calculated \((p - ) / (p - 1)\), \(p\) total number candidates \(\) candidate's rank. check (logical(1))  Whether run additional voter-integrity checks. TRUE, voter must approve least one candidate, approvals must unique per voter, approved candidates must appear candidates. Use FALSE skip checks inputs known valid.
